overall.description: A maritime simulation showing a first-person view from a ship's bridge at night. The vessel navigates through dark waters while the operator monitors radar, electronic charts, and depth sounder displays. The scene features continuous forward motion through a waterway with no other vessels visible.

events.description: vessel passes a navigation marker on the starboard side

agents.ego_description: operating the vessel from the bridge, monitoring navigation displays and radar while maintaining course through the waterway

entered.detail: electronic nautical chart showing vessel position and colored depth contours
circular radar screen with rotating sweep showing navigation targets
depth sounder showing underwater terrain profile
the bow of the ship with railings and deck lighting visible through the bridge windows
dark water surface illuminated by the vessel's forward lighting
structural frame of the bridge window on the port side
structural frame of the bridge window on the starboard side

segments.scene_description: The scene shows a continuous view from a ship's bridge at night, with the vessel moving steadily through dark waters. Navigation displays including radar, charts, and depth sounder are visible at the top of the frame, while the bow of the ship cuts through the water ahead. The camera remains fixed as the vessel passes a navigation marker on the right side around the 4-second mark.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a continuous first-person perspective from a ship's bridge as the vessel navigates through a dark, nocturnal waterway. It begins with a steady forward motion, where the bow of the ship cuts through deep black waters while navigation displays, including radar and electronic charts, remain visible at the top of the frame. As the vessel maintains its course, the camera captures the surrounding void of the night sea. The scene then shifts as a solitary navigation marker emerges from the darkness on the right side, passing by the bridge before the ship continues its journey into the open water.
